summary: Keep WTS requirement draft projection reviewable across the Workbench BFF and React contract fixtures.
rationale: The requirement projection adds structured draft sections, selectable items, confirmation state, and other-input prompt fields to the BFF contract. The generated schema and typed React fixtures must move in the same PR so Workbench Contract CI can validate this branch independently.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base codex/wts-04-problem-ops
deletion_targets: Requirement draft BFF fields that only become valid after a later schema-sync PR
React requirement fixtures that omit required draft review fields
Workbench stories that no longer match the generated requirement draft schema
risks: Requirement projection must stay recruiter-safe and avoid raw extraction payloads.
The generated schema must remain deterministic and reviewed with the BFF projection.
This PR should only update fixtures needed for contract compatibility, not the full interactive requirement review UI.
